data.title: Federal Trade Commission sets closed door meeting for next Thursday
data.description:
The Federal Trade Commission has scheduled a closed-door meeting for next Thursday as FTC Chair Lina Khan's reign nears an end.

The meeting, for the consideration of multiple nonpublic law enforcement matters, is scheduled for 1 pm on Jan. 16, according to a listing for the event on the FTC's website.

Investors often speculate when the meeting notices are given that the FTC may be voting on an ongoing deal that's being reviewed. While sometimes these meetings are about public companies, oftentimes they are on private firms or other matters. Potential deals that the FTC may be looking at include IBM's (IBM) purchase of HashiCorp (HCP), CoStar's (CSGP) acquisition of Matterport (MTTR), Surmodics (SRDX) sale to private equity firm GTCR or possibly Boeing's (BA) planned purchase of Spirit Aerosystems (SPR).

President-elect Donald Trump last month picked Andrew Ferguson to be the next chair of the FTC, replacing Khan.

data.title: Wiz names Merchant CFO as it nears IPO following busy 2024
data.description:
Wiz announced the appointment of Fazal Merchant as its new chief financial officer and president on Thursday as the cybersecurity startup expands its executive team and makes moves for an initial public offering within a year or so.

Merchant previously served as co-CEO of Tanium, a privately held cybersecurity firm located in Kirkland, Wash. He was also CFO at DreamWorks Animation.

The last year was busy for Wiz, a cloud security company headquartered in New York City with offices in Tel Aviv. In 2024, the company acquired Gem Security, a cloud detection and response startup, and Dazz, a security and risk management startup, for a combined $800M. The company also raised $1B in a funding round.

In July, Wiz walked away from a $23B acquisition deal with Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) and refocused its efforts on going public.

"As CFO & President, he will up-level our business with strategies and processes to help us mature and seize the opportunity that lies before us," said Wiz CEO and co-founder Assaf Rappaport, in a blog post. "With Fazal on board, the team will be better positioned than ever to navigate the road ahead while continuing to innovate on behalf of our customers."

data.title: After Santa Rally letdown, January advance 'key' to positive 2025
data.description:
Santa Claus delivered a loss during the “Santa Rally” period ushering in a new equity-trading year, but history shows a winning January can lay the groundwork for an S&P 500 (SP500) annual gain, the publisher of the Stock Trader's Almanac said.

The Santa Rally period produced a 0.5% decline from the end of trade on Dec. 23, 2024, through Jan. 3’s close. Now, investors are putting the so-called "January Barometer" in view. After seven Santa Rally failures going back to 1967, the S&P 500 (SP500) finished with positive annual returns in six of those following years after January gains.

18.2% was the average return in those seven years. The table below comes from AlmanacTrader posting on X (formerly Twitter):

The “January Barometer has got to be positive,” to set the stage for a 2025 advance, Jeffrey Hirsch, editor-in-chief of the Stock Trader's Almanac, told Fox Business this week. He noted his father and the publication’s creator, Yale Hirsch, created the Santa Rally and January Barometer indicators in 1972.

“It’s really all about January being the key here,” Hirsch said, adding the market this month will see the Trump administration coming into the White House. “So we’ll have a good read on what's going on in D.C., we'll have a better read on inflation in the coming weeks when some data comes out.”

The S&P 500 (SP500) can be tracked through ETFs including: (VOO), (SPY), (SDS), (UPRO), and (IVV).

Nasdaq (COMP:IND) and Dow (DJI) exchange-traded funds include: (QQQ), (TQQQ), (SQQQ), (DIA), (UDOW) and (DOG).

data.title: Apartment Investment Management expands review of options
data.description:
Apartment Investment Management's (NYSE:AIV) board will explore additional alternatives to further unlock shareholder value after it agreed to sell its Brickell Bay properties for ~$520M, the company known as Aimco said on Thursday.

The expanded review will consider such options as reducing exposure to development activity and monetizing certain assets. It may also include exploring the potential sales of the major parts of its business, an acceleration of individual asset sales, or a sale or merger of the company as a whole.

"Our guiding principle will be to produce an outcome that delivers maximum value to Aimco shareholders," the company said in a statement.

Aimco's (NYSE:AIV) investment committee, comprised of the board's four independent directors, is overseeing the process. Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC is serving as the company's financial adviser.

The company can't assure that that expanded strategic review will result in any transaction or other strategic changes.

In the past year, Aimco (AIV) shares rose 9.4%, lagging the S&P 500's 24% growth.

data.title: Bayer hot flashes therapy for breast cancer patients hits main goal in late-stage trial
data.description:
Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) (OTCPK:BAYRY) announced Thursday that elinzanetant, its experimental therapy for hot flashes, reached the main goals in a late-stage trial for women with or at a high risk of developing breast cancer.

Hot flashes represent a common adverse effect of endocrine therapy, a well-established adjuvant therapy for hormone-positive breast cancer.

Announcing topline data from its OASIS 4 Phase 3 study, the German conglomerate said that its non-hormonal treatment option compared to placebo cut the occurrence of hot flashes (vasomotor symptoms) with a statistically significant effect from Week 4 to Week 12.

OASIS 4, the fourth trial in the company's OASIS program to generate positive topline data, was designed to evaluate elinzanetant in treating hot flashes caused by endocrine therapy in women with or at a high risk of developing hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer.

Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) noted that the global trial of 474 patients also reached all secondary endpoints, including a measure related to the reductions in the severity of VMS, and added that the drug's safety profile over 52 weeks was consistent with findings from the prior trials.

"The positive results from OASIS 4 bring us one step closer to a much-needed non-hormonal option for managing VMS in breast cancer patients and women at risk of breast cancer," said Dr. Fatima Cardoso, OASIS 4's lead investigator.

Multiple regulatory submissions for elinzanetant are currently underway in the U.S. and other countries based on the company's OASIS program.

data.title: Restaurant Brands International has international plans for the Firehouse Subs chain
data.description:
Restaurant Brands International (NYSE:QSR) announced on Thursday the launch of its Firehouse Subs brand in Brazil, as the chain builds on its international expansion. The brand plans to open more than 500 restaurants in 10 years across the country. The first restaurant is expected to launch in 2025 and will mark Firehouse Subs debut in South America.

The Firehouse Subs expansion into Brazil is part of a joint venture between parent company Restaurant Brands International (NYSE:QSR) and Iuri Miranda, former CEO and board member of Zamp S.A., master franchisee of Burger King and Popeyes in Brazil. The joint venture is seen leveraging Restaurant Brands' (QSR) global infrastructure and expertise in digital strategy and international development. Firehouse Subs aims to capture Brazil's growing sandwich market.

Firehouse Subs was founded in 1994 by brothers Chris and Robin Sorensen, former firefighters from Jacksonville, Florida. Initially, the brothers attempted franchising in 1995, but soon decided to focus on company-owned stores. In 2000, they revisited franchising with a new approach, setting up financing for potential franchisees.

The chain experienced rapid growth, and by 2012 Firehouse Subs had reached 500 locations, and in 2016, it opened its 1,000th restaurant. The company expanded internationally, opening locations in Canada in 2015 and Switzerland in 2023. In 2021, Restaurant Brands International (QSR) acquired Firehouse Subs for $1 billion.

Firehouse Subs now boasts over 1,300 restaurants.

data.title: Euro stocks can offer a lesson in high concentration for the U.S. – Goldman
data.description:
European equities (NYSEARCA:VGK), (STOXX), (FEZ) provide a lesson about the risk of high concentration, according to Goldman Sachs.

The top seven companies by market cap in Europe had fallings in 2024 due to earnings disappointments, including Louis Vuitton (OTCPK:LVMHF), Novo Nordisk (NVO), and ASML Holdings (ASML).

Here they are with their current one-year performances:

  • Novo Nordisk (NVO) – Down 19.51% in a year
  • Louis Vuitton (OTCPK:LVMHF) – Down 13.91% in a year
  • ASML Holdings (ASML) – Up 3.21% in a year
  • SAP SE (SAP) – Up 66.16% in a year
  • Hermès International Société (OTCPK:HESAY) – Up 29.84% in a year
  • Accenture (ACN) – Up 5.11% in a year
  • L’Oreal S.A. (OTCPK:LRLCF) – Down 29.3% in a year

Consequently, they had a negative impact on the broader European market, said Peter Oppenheimer, chief global equity strategist at Goldman Sachs’s Global Investment Research, in a note.

The STOXX Europe 600 Index (STOXX) is up only 8% in a year, and down 1.11% from a month ago.

The Vanguard FTSE Europe ETF (NYSEARCA:VGK) is down 0.05% from a year ago, and down 5.24% from a month ago.

On the other hand, the rise in capex spending that mega-cap technology companies are making is reducing free cash flow and how much these companies will see their profits grow.

“For example, while the ‘Magnificent 7’ is expected to generate earnings growth of 33% in 2024, the rest of the S&P 500 (SP500) is only expected to grow 3%,” said Oppenheimer. “In 2025 and 2026, this gap is expected to narrow dramatically.”

“This makes the case for diversification more powerful,” he added, and recommended geographical diversification, broadening of sector exposures, and concentrating on “alpha” rather than “beta.”

data.title: IGM Biosciences cuts workforce, halts a couple of product developments
data.description:
• IGM Biosciences (NASDAQ:IGMS) announced that it is currently evaluating internal options as well as potential strategic alternatives.
  • Under these changes, it will halt further development of imvotamab, an IgM-based CD20 X CD3 bispecific antibody T cell engager, and IGM-2644, an IgM-based CD38 X CD3 bispecific antibody T cell engager, for autoimmune diseases.
  • IGMS is also reducing workforce by 73% to preserve cash.
  • The company reported cash and investments of approximately $183.8M as of December 31, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kansas City Fed's Schmid prefers to tread cautiously in further rate cuts
data.description:
After the Federal Reserve cut its policy rate by 100 basis points in three meetings, Kansas City Fed President Jeff Schmid favors moving cautiously in further cuts. "With inflation close to target and growth showing continued momentum, I believe we are near the point where the economy needs neither restriction nor support and that policy should be neutral," he said Thursday, according to prepared text for a speech.

Prices increased 2.5% over the past year, nearing the Fed's goal of 2%, and while the labor market has loosened, "it remains healthy," he said.

Schmid expects that the neutral rate — the point at which the interest rate neither boosts nor hinders economic growth — has increased since the pandemic. Last November, he argued that there are many reasons to expect that interest rates might settle at a higher neutral rate than before the pandemic, "including strong growth and investment demand, perhaps related to recent robust productivity growth, and the prospect of exciting new technologies like artificial intelligence."

"My read is that interest rates might be very close to their longer-run level now," he said. "Regardless, I am in favor of adjusting policy gradually going forward and only in response to a sustained change in the tone of the data. The strength of the economy allows us to be patient."

Schmid also spoke about continuing to reduce the Fed's balance sheet, reversing some of the actions it took to increase liquidity to support the economy during the pandemic. The Fed purchased ~$4.5T in Treasuries and mortgage-backed securities to support the economy. It has reduced its asset holdings by ~$2T from its peak.

He'd like to see the holdings drop by even more. "Shrinking our footprint will lessen our distortion of asset prices," he said. Furthermore, the Fed's holdings are weighted towards longer duration assets, which continues to push down long-term interest rates. "I would prefer to shift the composition of our balance sheet into shorter duration assets to minimize our impact," Schmid said. Also, he wants to move towards only holding Treasuries, which means shedding the mortgage-backed securities now on the balance sheet.

data.title: Mattel and TOKYOPOP to create manga comic books featuring Hot Wheels and Barbie
data.description:
Mattel (NASDAQ:MAT) and TOKYOPOP are teaming up through a publishing partnership that will develop manga-style graphic novels with Mattel’s (NASDAQ:MAT) two most popular toys.

Featuring Hot Wheels and Barbie, TOKYOPOP will create content for children between 8 and 12 years old.

“TOKYOPOP’s catalog is renowned for bestselling titles featuring notable characters for readers of all ages. Adding Barbie and Hot Wheels to our roster brings an exciting dimension, and we look forward to sharing these legendary brands and bold new adventures to our global audience of readers,” said TOKYOPOP COO and publisher Marc Visnick.

Manga is a distinct art form very similar to anime except manga is used primarily in comic books and graphic novels while anime is used for shows and movies.

The first titles will be released in 2026 and will feature characters from Barbie in a western-style graphic novel inspired by Hot Wheels.

The partnership between Mattel (NASDAQ:MAT) and TOKYOPOP is the latest effort by the toy manufacturer to further engage its fans through storytelling.
